name: Exercise 2: Automated Planning
postCreateCommand: pip install debugpy && sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y cmake g++ coinor-libcbc-dev coinor-libclp-dev coinor-libcoinutils-dev bison flex && git clone https://github.com/XayahSuSuSu/popf.git /tmp/popf && cd /tmp/popf && mkdir -p compile && cd compile && cmake ../src -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release && make -j$(nproc) popf3-clp && sudo cp /tmp/popf/compile/popf2/popf3-clp /usr/local/bin/popf-clp && sudo chmod +x /usr/local/bin/popf-clp && rm -rf /tmp/popf && echo 'POPF planner installed successfully.' && if [ "$(uname -m)" = "x86_64" ]; then curl -fsSL -o /tmp/val-linux64.zip 'https://dev.azure.com/schlumberger/4e6bcb11-cd68-40fe-98a2-e3777bfec0a6/_apis/build/builds/77/artifacts?artifactName=linux64&api-version=5.2-preview.5&$format=zip' && unzip -o /tmp/val-linux64.zip -d /tmp/val-extract && sudo mkdir -p /opt/val/bin && sudo cp /tmp/val-extract/linux64/* /opt/val/bin/ && sudo chmod +x /opt/val/bin/* && rm -rf /tmp/val-linux64.zip /tmp/val-extract && echo 'VAL tools installed successfully.'; else echo 'Note: VAL tools are only available on x86_64 (GitHub Codespaces). You can still use the online planner (Alt+P) for testing your PDDL files.'; fi
